metadata.source_note: Official Hawaii resident individual income tax instructions. Printed page 33 (PDF page 35) states the complete Tax on Capital Gains Worksheet algorithm, including Hawaii long-term and short-term adjustments and the required smaller-of comparisons. This 2025 worksheet is retained for structural use only: its printed line-12 amounts of $24,000, $36,000, and $48,000 are incorrect for tax year 2026. Hawaii DOTAX's official April 22, 2026 correction notice replaces them with $48,000 for single or married filing separately, $72,000 for head of household, and $96,000 for joint or qualifying surviving spouse. Those corrected amounts are also the taxable-income ceilings below 7.25 percent in the HRS 235-51(a)-(c) schedules applicable to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2024; HRS 235-51(f) supplies the 7.25 percent alternative rate. Use corpus citation us-hi/statute/235-51 for 2026 amounts.
metadata.title: Hawaii Form N-11 Instructions, Tax Year 2025 — Tax on Capital Gains Worksheet
